title: Mirabilis linearis
taxonomy: Mirabilis linearis is classified in the family Nyctaginaceae. This family consists of desert plants and shrubs, with some species being popular for their ornamental value. As a member of the Nyctaginaceae family, Mirabilis linearis is further classified within the genus Mirabilis. The genus Mirabilis is a group of flowering plants commonly known as four o'clocks. These plants are native to the Americas, with many species found in dry areas.
uses: Mirabilis linearis, also known as the linear four o'clock, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant's roots are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The roots are often made into a tea or tincture to facilitate their medicinal effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.

intro: Mirabilis linearis, also known as Four-wing Saltbrush or Desert Four-wing Saltbrush, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Mirabilis linearis include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is sometimes employed in traditional remedies for colds, fever, and digestive issues.
